queries.question: What is true about Gaz International?

options.A: It is a Russian-owned company.
options.B: It took control of another company.
options.C: Its stock price has slipped due to a false rumor.
options.D: It was involved in a multi-million dollar transaction recently.

queries.question: Which of the following will be the result of a deal mentioned in the article?

options.A: Gaz International will become the leading company in its field.
options.B: Yevgeni Star seems to have gotten the better end of the deal.
options.C: Gaz International will now focus on establishing its business in new areas.
options.D: Yevgeni Star will distribute shares to its top management staff.

queries.question: Who is Mr. Fritz Faschier?

options.A: A reporter for a local newspaper
options.B: The president of Gaz International
options.C: A spokesperson for Yevgeni Star

queries.question: According to the article, when will the deal be finalized?

options.B: In five years
options.C: Within the following weeks

queries.question: What is the main cause of the delay in closing the deal?

options.A: Both companies want to take it slow.
options.B: Some complications have surfaced regarding legal issues.
options.C: The Russian authorities usually take a long time to process a new license.
options.D: There are many details to be worked out.

queries.question: What is the purpose of the announcement?

options.A: To restrict unnecessary staff members from visiting the work area
options.B: To provide a timetable for some office renovation work
options.C: To thank employees for their support in the work
options.D: To prepare for an unexpected shipment delay

queries.question: Where will employees on the ground floor likely be on March 15?

options.A: The first floor
options.B: The second floor
options.C: The third floor

queries.question: The word "urge" in paragraph 2, line 2 is closest in meaning to

options.A: influence
options.B: encourage
options.C: support

queries.question: During which period will the employees on the fourth floor not be affected?

options.A: April 11-20
options.B: April 21-30
options.C: May 1-9

queries.question: What is being reported?

options.A: The slow rise of the desktop division to the top of its field
options.B: The listing of a computer company on the stock market
options.C: The new spending budget for GEM Computer's R&D Department
options.D: The selling of a struggling division of GEM Computers

queries.question: Why did GEM Computers make its decision to make the move?

options.A: Electronica made an offer they couldn't refuse.
options.B: The company was on the verge of bankruptcy.
options.C: The division was not making enough profits.
options.D: The demand for computer desktops was low.

queries.question: How is the news being interpreted by the market?

options.A: GEM will soon become obsolete.
options.B: GEM got the best of the deal.
options.C: Electronica will start making a lot of money.
options.D: The desktop division will receive more funds.

queries.question: Who is the recipient of this delivery?

options.A: Mr. Mitchell
options.B: Mr. Lee
options.C: Mr. Chow

queries.question: How should the delivery be handled if it does not reach the recipient?

options.A: Return it to the sender right away
options.B: Resend it to an alternate address
options.C: Keep it at the post office for safekeeping

queries.question: Which of the following information is NOT in the document?

options.A: The shipping cost
options.B: The delivery date
options.C: The number of items

queries.question: What is suggested in the advertisement?

options.A: The store will be celebrating its third year of business.
options.B: The price of items in the store will be slashed roughly by one third.
options.C: The sale period will last for two months.
options.D: The discounts will only be available to premier members.

queries.question: How can a customer receive the largest discount?

options.A: By coming in early to the store
options.B: By purchasing two or more items
options.C: By signing up for a special membership card

queries.question: What problem is mentioned in the memo?

options.A: A meeting had to be rescheduled.
options.B: A conference room is unavailable.
options.C: Spending had to be reduced.

queries.question: What is Renée Walker expected to do on Friday?

options.A: Approve a budget
options.B: Order equipment
options.C: Attend a theater performance

queries.question: How much will Ms. Parker pay for domestic long distance calls?
What does the phone bill imply?

options.A: Ms. Parker makes most of her calls from her cell phone.
options.B: The long distance calls are cheaper on the weekends.
options.C: There is a fixed rate on the telephone bill every month.
options.D: Angelina spends a lot of time surfing the Internet.

queries.question: Which of the following service will NOT be available in July?

options.A: Faster Internet connection
options.B: Speedier international calls
options.C: Quicker technical service

queries.question: Starting in July, from which service will the users benefit?

options.A: International calls
options.B: Domestic calls
options.C: Internet access

queries.question: Looking at the telephone bill, which service will likely affect Ms. Parker the least in July?

options.A: Internet access
options.B: International phone calls
options.C: Domestic calls to cell phones

queries.question: According to the weather forecast in the first article, what is true?

options.A: The city would go through unprecedented temperatures.
options.B: The rainstorm would begin on Thursday morning.
options.C: The weather would turn sunny as soon as the rain stopped.
options.D: The rain would last for the whole month.

queries.question: In the second article, what problem did Wasniak’s Parks and Recreation Department experience?

options.A: It couldn’t get enough funding from the city government.
options.B: Its regional parking lots were flooded by rain.
options.C: It had to cancel all of its scheduled events.

queries.question: Why is August an important month for the Parks and Recreation Department?

options.A: It posts its yearly schedule of events.
options.B: It postpones and cancels many events during that month.
options.C: It generates a good portion of their yearly revenue.
options.D: It provides the best weather for plant growth.

queries.question: In the second article, the word “maintenance” in paragraph 2, line 3 is closest in meaning to

options.A: consistency.
options.B: upkeep
options.C: defense

queries.question: What were the forecasters correct about?

options.A: The sky was overcast.
options.B: The temperature
options.C: The rain
